sys_metadata_link.payload:
true text 89137fd0db4420106a58ef92ca961903 false false false false false 6a0e0e96db4d99501e1792c5d4961956 ddba8256db4d99501e1792c5d496195a This list displays the Tasker users who have the Tasker role for the group and are added to a Tasker KB0010007 2022-08-09 Member Table Roles Count kb_knowledge rjoy 2022-08-09 22:43:39 global / bbd9395cdb1111901e1792c5d49619ed 2 csloyer 2022-09-22 15:32:51 19 This list displays the Tasker users who have the Tasker role for the group and are added to a Tasker Organizational Unit (OU) as a member. When there is a mismatch between ServiceNow Role Members and Tasker Member Table Roles, the “Let’s fix it” message displays. This is not necessarily an error, as users can be assigned a role through group permissions but not assigned to a Tasker OU.

“Let’s fix it” also displays when removing a user from a Tasker group – which removes the role from the ServiceNow Role Members table, but not Tasker Member Table Roles.

Selecting “Let’s fix it” synchronizes the users listed in the Tasker Member Table Role users with the ServiceNow Role Members.

The fields in the list are fields from the Tasker Member OU member record. Selecting a field opens the Organizational Member record for the user.

  • Default Member OU is the user’s default Organizational Unit (OU).
  • Member User is the user selected on the member record.
  • Title displays the title from the user’s ServiceNow user record / Tasker user profile.
  • Global Hierarchy Administrator indicates if the role is enabled (true or false).
  • Template Designer Permissions Administrator indicates if the role is enabled (true or false).
  • Task Administrator indicates if the role is enabled (true or false).
  • Tasker Application Administrator indicates if the role is enabled (true or false).
  • Context Menu: Selecting the content menu displays options to view the Organizational Members list in ServiceNow and options for exporting to PDF, Excel, and CSV.
]]> General 0 2100-01-01 published
